post:
Micron Technology is benefiting from a severe memory chip shortage that has pushed DRAM prices to their highest levels since 2019, prompting Phillip Capital to initiate coverage with a Buy rating. Demand for Micron’s high-bandwidth memory remains strong, with HBM capacity sold out through 2026 alongside market leader SK Hynix. 
Supply constraints across the industry and sustained AI infrastructure investment are driving DRAM price increases, which are expected to accelerate in fiscal 2026. Micron’s HBM3E products are already designed into Nvidia’s Blackwell GPUs and AMD’s MI355 GPUs, supporting strong revenue growth. 
Phillip Capital highlighted Micron’s next-generation HBM4 as a potential inflection point, with ramp-up expected in the second quarter of 2026 and projected speeds exceeding 11 gigabits per second per pin, potentially enabling market share gains versus SK Hynix as volumes scale.

author: dojidad
post:
$AMD There are a few things I am looking at before adding to my position, or what I would notice before taking a position. This is a strong candle. It's called a bullish Engulfing Candle because the body of this candle is completely engulfing the previous candles body. That's usually a good sign. 
 #1, the 5-day EMA (green line) is under ther 10-day SMA (yellow line). If this reverses I would look at adding to my position, or if I wasn't in it, starting a position because the 5-day trending above the 10-day means momentum is probably changing to positive  
#2. the bottom wick touched and bounced off the 21 (orange line). The 21 is called the pivot moving average, and it's a good place for a stock to pivot off of. Of course, ER is coming up, so it depends on your strategy.

☁️ $ORCL makes a bold cloud bet 
 
Oracle shares are up ~3.6% premarket after announcing plans to raise $45–50B in 2026 to massively expand cloud capacity and meet AI demand from $NVDA, $META, $AMD, OpenAI, TikTok, and xAI. 
 
📉 The move comes with baggage. $ORCL is still ~50% below its September peak, after roughly $460B in market cap was wiped out as investors worried about aggressive AI spending and leverage. 
 
💰 The capital raise will blend debt and equity, including up to $20B in at-the-market stock sales and equity-linked securities. Management expects free cash flow to stay negative into 2030 due to massive data center and chip commitments. 
 
⚖️ Bulls see Oracle locking in AI cloud demand early. Bears see dilution and balance-sheet risk. Either way, Oracle is going all-in on the cloud.

post:
PickAlpha Weekend View-

Oracle says it plans to raise $45B–$50B via debt + equity to fund more cloud capacity for AI-era contracts (named customers include Nvidia, Meta Platforms, Advanced Micro Devices, OpenAI, xAI, and TikTok). The company is effectively acknowledging the funding scale needed to “feed AI,” while its stock is still ~50% off the peak and the market is focused on whether AI capex actually pays back.

Tickers: $ORCL $NVDA $AMD

Our view is this is a balance-sheet stress test, not a hype headline: ORCL is trying to bridge years of heavy capex with equity-linked issuance to protect its IG profile, but that also flags dilution + funding-risk as the market’s real bottleneck. The clean tell: if ORCL can’t place the equity-linked/ATM smoothly (or spreads re-widen), the market will treat “contracted demand” as lower-quality AI demand and re-rate the stock; if the raise clears with contained dilution, ORCL buys time and keeps the AI capacity flywheel alive.
